title: acmella grandiflora
intro: Acmella grandiflora, also known as Parlor Plant or Sensitive Plant, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family, characterized by its delicate white flowers and sensitive leaves that fold up when touched. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Acmella grandiflora include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and soothing digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Acmella grandiflora can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
